name: Indole-3-propionic acid improved aged-mouse muscle and bone measures and extended fly lifespan in subsets
summary: The 2026 IPA study provides direct animal healthspan and invertebrate lifespan support for microbial tryptophan metabolites.
statement: In aged mice, indole-3-propionic acid treatment for 12 weeks improved grip strength and hang time and enhanced bone microarchitecture, while Drosophila lifespan extension was significant but genotype- and sex-specific.
population_or_model: Aged C57BL/6 mice and Drosophila melanogaster
time_horizon: 12 weeks in aged mice and full lifespan in Drosophila
quantitative_note: The abstract reports IPA 20 mg/kg subcutaneously three times per week in aged mice and 100 microM in Drosophila food.
caveats: Mouse treatment was subcutaneous rather than a gut-restoration strategy.
The lifespan effect was in Drosophila and depended on genotype and sex.
No human direct IPA supplementation or functional aging trial is established.
